mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or signal loses predictive power when unemployment growth stays below 0.15pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression or forward returns turn negative when both series rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: Signal reverses direction during tightening cycles or 5-day return frequency falls below 50% when INDPRO trends upward.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index levels above 250 combined with federal funds rates exceeding 2.5 percent create stagflationary pressures that compress profit margins and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances or the joint threshold fails to separate positive from negative return periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in >55% of occurrences or the interaction effect vanishes when payroll growth falls below 100k.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly gross domestic product values indicate stronger aggregate economic output, which translates into positive short-term market sentiment and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with GDP levels or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in both industrial production and retail sales signal a synchronized contraction in supply and demand, triggering recession fears and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for 2 consecutive months and RSAFS declines for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or the dual-decline threshold fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance.
